summary: Iteration 4 verified end-to-end. Backend: 29/29 pytest pass ‚Äî new tests cover POST /applications with optional discord_user_id (stored + returned in admin list), GET /applications/status/{ref} for full UUID + 8-char prefix + case-insensitive + 404 (missing) + 400 (too short), PATCH /admin/applications/{id} with token guard (401), invalid status (400), missing id (404), and full status transitions (accepted ‚Üí interview ‚Üí rejected ‚Üí pending). Fire-and-forget Discord webhook + bot DM do not crash the request even with an invalid discord_user_id. Frontend (Playwright on preview URL): /apply now has the optional 'Discord User ID' field, submitting without it succeeds and the success card shows large 8-char REF, copy/status/another buttons; navbar 'Status' link routes to /status; /status accepts lowercase ref and renders status card, bogus ref shows error block; /admin Applications tab renders status badges (PENDING/ACCEPTED), the 'Discord User ID (for DM)' cell, message textarea, and Accept/Interview/Reject/Re-open buttons ‚Äî Accept transitions card to ACCEPTED with re-open visible; Re-open returns to PENDING and hides itself; 'Marked ...' toasts appear (no DM-sent hint when discord_user_id is absent, matching spec).

issues: When ref < 4 chars the validation error appears but the previously-rendered status card is NOT cleared (handler returns before calling setResult(null)). Minor UX nit ‚Äî old result and new error are shown together.
Re-open button uses white/20 border on dark transparent bg ‚Äî borderline low contrast vs the strong Accept/Reject buttons. Functional, but visually subordinate which may be intentional.

action_items: Optional polish: in StatusPage.submit(), call setResult(null) BEFORE the length-check early-return so the stale card disappears when the user types a too-short ref.
Consider hiding/clearing the status-error block when typing in the ref input (currently sticky until next submit).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py admin_update_application_status: status_value compared after .lower().strip() but never written back canonical ‚Äî fine since payload is normalised before update.

DM body and webhook embed are fire-and-forget via asyncio.create_task ‚Äî task exceptions only logged at the requests level inside the thread executor; an exception escaping the helper would be silently dropped by the task. Acceptable for non-critical side-effect, but consider task.add_done_callback or try/except in the async wrapper for observability.
STATUS_LABELS uses 'RE-OPENED (PENDING)' for pending ‚Äî DM is only sent when status != 'pending' (good), but the webhook embed IS still sent on re-open (matches spec, just noting).

Application response model now includes review_message/reviewed_at; admin list endpoint coerces submitted_at but does NOT coerce reviewed_at ‚Äî Pydantic still accepts the ISO string for datetime, but for consistency consider coercing both.

Frontend StatusPage strips non-hex chars from input before lookup ‚Äî that's fine for UUIDs but means if backend ever changes ref format (e.g., adds letters g-z) the client filter would silently break it. Low-risk for now.
Carried over from prior iters: deprecated @app.on_event startup/shutdown handlers (FastAPI lifespan is recommended).

success_rate.frontend: 100% (apply success, navbar Status link, /status lookup + error, admin status transitions accept‚Üíre-open)

seed_data_creation: Backend tests created 2 TEST_ applications (TEST_Iter4 Applicant w/ discord_user_id='000000000000000001', TEST_Iter4 NoDiscordID). Playwright created 1 more (TEST_PW Iter4, REF 69BADD65). All have TEST_ prefix and remain in DB harmlessly; cycled the first through accepted‚Üíinterview‚Üírejected‚Üípending. No cleanup performed (no DELETE endpoint for applications).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 4 test file is /app/backend/tests/test_pillbox_iter4.py ‚Äî kept separate from iter2's test_pillbox_api.py for clarity. Both run cleanly together (29 tests, ~2.5s). DISCORD_WEBHOOK_URL and DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN are LIVE in backend/.env, but backend never blocks on or fails the API call because of Discord errors ‚Äî outbound 401/404 from Discord are expected when the bot can't DM arbitrary users. Re-open button only appears when status != 'pending' (verified). To find a specific app card use selector [data-testid="app-{first-8-of-uuid-lowercase}"]. There are ~28 TEST_ applications now in the DB from accumulated iterations ‚Äî fine, but if the dataset matters add a /api/admin/applications/{id} DELETE endpoint.
rca of the issue: N/A ‚Äî no bugs found. All iteration-4 acceptance criteria from the review request satisfied.
